consistency.level_school_match: Consistent. Level 6 Conjuration is plausible for this effect.
consistency.casting_time_duration: Consistent. A 1-minute setup for a permanent (until dispelled) utility effect fits standard D&D spell pacing.
consistency.material_component_usage: Consistent. The material component (sapphire) is integral to the spell's function (inscription and trigger) and its high cost (1,000 gp) matches the utility of instantaneous retrieval across planes.

quality.description_clarity: High. The description clearly outlines the setup, the trigger condition (crushing the sapphire), and the two outcomes (successful retrieval or location reveal if held by another).
quality.flavor: High. The concept of binding an object's identity to a costly gem for immediate retrieval is flavorful and mechanically sound for a high-level spell.
quality.balance_considerations: The 1,000 gp cost per use limits spamming and balances the power of instantaneous, planar object retrieval. The 'Dispel magic' counter is a standard balancing factor.

mechanics.trigger_mechanism: Action to speak the name and crush the sapphire. This is clear.
mechanics.range_and_scope: Touch for casting, but the retrieval range is effectively infinite ('regardless of physical or planar distances').
mechanics.limitations: Strict limits on item size (10 lbs, 6 ft max dimension) prevent summoning massive objects or creatures.
mechanics.interaction_with_creatures: The contingency clause (learning location if another holds it) is an excellent mechanical feature, turning a failed retrieval into useful intelligence.
